economic_notes: Nation operates a Video Lottery Terminal (VLT) program and a Convenience Store and Gas Bar. Construction in progress includes CMHC Section 95 housing units and new ISC housing project. Nation participates in First Nations Finance Authority borrowing and First Peoples Economic Growth Fund programs.
notes: Nation consolidates 5 entities: Administration, VLT Program, Health Services, Convenience Store and Gas Bar, and Housing Authority. Net debt position of $19,871,444. Large increase in CMHC funding from $779,901 to $4,735,886 due to new housing projects. Significant increase in tangible capital assets from $46,033,518 to $62,552,651 due to $19,398,686 in additions (primarily band buildings). Deferred revenue nearly doubled to $4,953,106, largely ISC deferred amounts. Construction in progress of $5,929,466 for incomplete CMHC and ISC housing projects. No schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text. Community and Economic Development segment included in expenses_infrastructure field as a proxy; actual segment breakdown: Administration $2,311,510, Social Assistance $5,898,425, Community Services $2,446,119, Capital Projects $5,331,835, Education $993,475, Community Wellness $7,280,706, Community and Economic Development $5,110,252, Housing $1,198,771.
